75795.subject: Weekly Recap: Wall Street's Onchain Sprint
75795.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

This was the week **Circle** won a national trust-bank charter, placing USDC reserves under federal bank supervision, while **Hyperliquid** and **Phantom** asked the CFTC to write DeFi's non-custodial line into a formal rule.

Away from the regulators, **Vitalik Buterin** laid out a three-to-four-year "Lean Ethereum" overhaul, and **Robinhood**'s week-old chain spent its debut overrun by memecoins, a long way from the tokenized stocks it was built for.

Enjoy!

———————————————————————————

The most decentralized staking protocol just got a big upgrade. Saturn One improves Rocket Pool's capital efficiency, helping node operators stake with less ETH while increasing capacity for liquid stakers. **[Now is the best time to stake your ETH with Rocket Pool](https://rocketpool.net/?utm_source=defiant&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=weekly-recap)**.

———————————————————————————

———————————————————————————

##### SPONSOR

## Building Paragon: A Perps Market on Hyperliquid

Youtube: 🟢 LIVE: Building Paragon: Perps Market on Hyperliquid (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f-aO3MJoRzU)

This week's build session goes inside **Paragon**, a new perpetuals market launching on **Hyperliquid** — the same venue whose policy arm spent the week petitioning the CFTC. **Camila Russo** [sits down with the team to walk through the design live](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f-aO3MJoRzU).

———————————————————————————

# Top Stories This Week

## Circle Wins Final OCC Approval for a National Trust Bank

**Circle** received [final OCC approval to charter a national trust bank](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/circle-wins-final-occ-approval-for-national-trust-bank), First National Digital Currency Bank, letting it custody digital assets and eventually hold the reserves backing **USDC** under direct federal banking supervision. It is the strongest regulatory footing any major stablecoin issuer has secured; CRCL shares rose more than 10% on the news.

## Hyperliquid and Phantom Ask the CFTC to Exempt DeFi From Broker Rules

**Hyperliquid**'s policy arm and wallet maker **Phantom** [filed a joint comment with the CFTC](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/hyperliquid-phantom-ask-cftc-to-exempt-defi-from-broker-rules) seeking to exempt non-custodial wallets and on-chain infrastructure from exchange and broker registration requirements. The filing would turn the no-action relief Phantom won in March into a formal rule for every provider that never takes custody of user funds.

## Vitalik Buterin Outlines a Multi-Year 'Lean Ethereum' Overhaul

**Vitalik Buterin** [laid out a 'Lean Ethereum' roadmap](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/vitalik-buterin-outlines-lean-ethereum-roadmap-a-three-to-four-year-protocol-overhaul), a three-to-four-year protocol overhaul aimed at simplifying Ethereum's base layer and hardening it for the long term. The plan reorients the roadmap around fewer, cleaner core components as the network

75793.subject: The world’s most liveable cities in 2026
75793.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzR9AAcAAAAAA9gfX8HM6Vs7Bs84nqXygJNHjSXiQIRgW1919AtKu51tYLNA0A5_ZmLbGZ_olGRUoh5VVsyuYG1OcwBj9dCiHmwuGBBNJe-Sci2mdzm0bayhPg 

July 11th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist This Weekend

 


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzR9AAcAAAAAA9gfX8HNdNcYR8LlbT4ribTVRpAQv0DWeQC78B_VTQzV-ORQEWPh976X2Yt3dKJbvsg5F1auP-j0UF-PucfznCb-dHK6P0IN1zrs7aEZlwJoGw 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzR9AAcAAAAAA9gfX8HOTjzqzvtVx9kvQzSqB5DWf9koBh4fKE-TerXtQSUIIGRayFgKlnliN6_pOOCN3kLXhaNtApMNpd6IO95J5_IDcwtVA-kvEXxT6Vlvzw 


 


https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzR9AAcAAAAAA9gfX8HPt2svzw6aKnsQ329B-pQnK--nMnznIoEH5ezGedcihTpmdvO-OS3uw_MQ_9zAPPijITx0GzAW2TqU6Nk7Rh_wi2saB40CGqYY 


Alex Selby-Boothroyd

Head of data journalism



A year ago in this newsletter I wrote, “It will be a relief if the [liveable-cities] index in 2026 shows that the world remains as liveable as it is now.” In a way I got my wish: the latest global average liveability score held steady at 76.1 out of 100, the same as in 2025. But drill down into the regions and 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzR9AAcAAAAAA9gfX8HQMHsXmkAmB5yB7_3ryKCAd2ZgLzkYYcETp06njKdjgiOHbxpMvuenPPb6papwh2xofArPMO3vJuz8rzYPWaL3_3tOF7rENLuI 
it looks less stable .


Cities across the Middle East, from Doha to Dubai, dropped down the ranki
